categories.label: Copyright & IP
categories.definition: Content that infringes copyright, trademarks, or intellectual-property rights of others — including full-work reproduction, sync misuse, and counterfeit sales.

subcategories.label: Unauthorized full-work reproduction
subcategories.definition: A full copy of a copyrighted work uploaded without rights.
subcategories.example: A full-length film uploaded to a public feed with no license documentation.

subcategories.label: Sync / soundtrack infringement
subcategories.definition: Licensed music used in a video without sync or mechanical rights.
subcategories.example: A popular song playing over a montage with no music license attached.

subcategories.label: Franchise / character IP misuse
subcategories.definition: Unauthorized use of a recognizable fictional character or franchise in promotional or derivative content.
subcategories.example: A creator selling merchandise featuring a well-known animated character without a license.

subcategories.label: Trademark / logo infringement
subcategories.definition: Displaying a protected brand logo or trademark in a way that implies false endorsement or affiliation.
subcategories.example: A video thumbnail placing a major tech company logo next to an unrelated product.

subcategories.label: Counterfeit / bootleg sales
subcategories.definition: Promoting or selling imitation goods that copy a protected brand's design or packaging.
subcategories.example: A video advertisement offering cheap replicas of a luxury handbag brand.

subcategories.label: Watermark removal / stripped credits
subcategories.definition: Removing or obscuring ownership watermarks, credits, or attribution from creative work.
subcategories.example: A stock-photo watermark cropped out of an image used in a promotional video.

subcategories.label: Livestream / event piracy
subcategories.definition: Re-broadcasting a paid or restricted live event without authorization.
subcategories.example: A restream of a pay-per-view sports match on a free video platform.

categories.label: Hate & Harassment
categories.definition: Content that attacks, demeans, or threatens individuals or groups based on protected characteristics, or that targets specific people with sustained abuse.

subcategories.label: Hate speech targeting protected characteristics
subcategories.definition: Expressions of contempt or inferiority directed at a group based on race, religion, gender, disability, or other protected traits.
subcategories.example: A monologue claiming a specific ethnic group is inherently inferior.

subcategories.label: Slurs / dehumanizing language
subcategories.definition: Using recognized slurs or language that compares people to animals, diseases, or subhuman categories.
subcategories.example: A comment section filled with racial slurs directed at the video creator.

subcategories.label: Targeted harassment / bullying of an individual
subcategories.definition: Repeated, directed abusive behavior toward a specific person, including threats, insults, or coordinated pile-ons.
subcategories.example: A series of videos mocking and insulting a specific private individual by name.

subcategories.label: Doxxing / PII exposure
subcategories.definition: Publishing private personal information — such as home address, phone number, or workplace — without consent.
subcategories.example: A video displaying a person's home address and encouraging viewers to visit.

subcategories.label: Threats of violence
subcategories.definition: Explicit or credible statements of intent to cause physical harm to a person or group.
subcategories.example: A creator stating on camera they will physically harm another public figure.

subcategories.label: Hateful groups / symbols / praise
subcategories.definition: Promoting, glorifying, or displaying symbols associated with hate organizations or extremist movements.
subcategories.example: A video featuring a recognized hate-group flag with approving commentary.

categories.label: Violence & Graphic Content
categories.definition: Content depicting or encouraging real-world physical harm, graphic injury, or dangerous acts that risk serious injury or death.

subcategories.label: Graphic real-world violence
subcategories.definition: Unedited footage of real assaults, shootings, or violent crimes.
subcategories.example: Security-camera footage of an armed robbery uploaded without context or warning.

subcategories.label: Gore / injury / medical graphic imagery
subcategories.definition: Close-up imagery of severe injuries, surgical procedures, or bodily trauma presented without educational framing.
subcategories.example: A close-up video of a battlefield wound shared for shock value.

subcategories.label: Animal abuse / cruelty
subcategories.definition: Content showing intentional harm, neglect, or mistreatment of animals.
subcategories.example: A video of a person deliberately injuring a domestic animal.

subcategories.label: Incitement to violence
subcategories.definition: Rhetoric that explicitly calls for, encourages, or provides instructions for violent acts against others.
subcategories.example: A speech urging a crowd to attack a specific building or person.

subcategories.label: Dangerous acts / stunts likely to cause injury
subcategories.definition: Challenges or stunts with a high risk of serious physical harm, especially when imitable by viewers.
subcategories.example: A video encouraging viewers to jump from a moving vehicle as a challenge.

subcategories.label: Weapons — instructional / brandishing
subcategories.definition: Demonstrating how to use, modify, or build weapons, or displaying weapons in a threatening manner.
subcategories.example: A tutorial on converting a legal item into an improvised weapon.

subcategories.label: Terrorism / violent extremism
subcategories.definition: Content produced by, promoting, or recruiting for designated terrorist or violent extremist organizations.
subcategories.example: A propaganda video from a recognized extremist group shared on a mainstream platform.

categories.label: Sexual & Nudity
categories.definition: Content depicting explicit sexual activity, nudity, or sexually suggestive material that may violate platform standards or age-gate requirements.

subcategories.label: Explicit sexual activity
subcategories.definition: Visual depiction of sexual intercourse or other explicit sexual acts.
subcategories.example: An unsimulated sexual encounter uploaded to a public video feed.

subcategories.label: Nudity (adult)
subcategories.definition: Full or partial nudity in a non-educational, non-artistic context involving adults.
subcategories.example: A creator appearing fully nude in a non-documentary video.

subcategories.label: Sexually suggestive content
subcategories.definition: Content that is not explicitly sexual but uses poses, framing, or language to evoke sexual arousal.
subcategories.example: A video with overtly sexual captions and provocative posing, though no nudity is shown.

subcategories.label: Non-consensual intimate imagery
subcategories.definition: Sharing intimate or sexual images of a person without their consent, including deepfakes.
subcategories.example: A video containing private intimate footage of an ex-partner shared as retaliation.

subcategories.label: Sexual solicitation / commercial sexual services
subcategories.definition: Offering, requesting, or facilitating paid sexual services or explicit content sales.
subcategories.example: A video description linking to a paid explicit-content subscription with direct solicitation.

subcategories.label: Fetish content likely to violate site policy
subcategories.definition: Content depicting niche sexual fetishes that, while not explicitly prohibited by law, exceed platform community norms.
subcategories.example: A video focused on a specific body-part fetish with no broader context.

categories.label: Minor Safety
categories.definition: Content that sexualizes, endangers, or exploits minors in any form, or that exposes children to adult themes without appropriate safeguards.

subcategories.label: CSAM / suspected child sexual abuse material
subcategories.definition: Any visual depiction of sexually explicit conduct involving a minor — illegal in all jurisdictions.
subcategories.example: A video containing explicit imagery of a child; report immediately to NCMEC and law enforcement.

subcategories.label: Child sexualization (non-CSAM)
subcategories.definition: Content that depicts minors in a sexualized manner without meeting the legal threshold of CSAM but still violating platform policy.
subcategories.example: A video of a minor performing suggestive dances in revealing clothing.

subcategories.label: Grooming / predatory behavior toward minors
subcategories.definition: Adults initiating inappropriate relationships with minors, including requesting private communication or images.
subcategories.example: An adult creator repeatedly asking a minor follower to message them privately.

subcategories.label: Minors in dangerous / harmful situations
subcategories.definition: Children depicted in situations that risk physical or psychological harm, including exploitation for entertainment.
subcategories.example: A video showing a child performing a dangerous stunt at an adult's direction.

subcategories.label: Minors + adult themes co-appearance
subcategories.definition: Children present in scenes involving violence, substance use, or sexual content, even if not the direct subject.
subcategories.example: A vlog where a child is visible in the background during an adult-themed discussion.

subcategories.label: PII disclosure of a minor
subcategories.definition: Publishing a minor's personal information — full name, school, home address, or contact details.
subcategories.example: A parent's video revealing their child's school name and daily route.

categories.label: Regulated Goods & Activities
categories.definition: Content promoting the sale, distribution, or use of goods and activities that are subject to legal regulation, including firearms, drugs, and age-restricted substances.

subcategories.label: Firearms / weapons sales
subcategories.definition: Advertising or facilitating the sale of firearms, ammunition, or explosive devices outside regulated channels.
subcategories.example: A video listing unregistered firearms for sale with contact information.

subcategories.label: Illicit drugs — sale / promotion
subcategories.definition: Promoting, instructing, or facilitating the sale or manufacture of controlled substances.
subcategories.example: A video tutorial on synthesizing a controlled substance at home.

subcategories.label: Alcohol / tobacco / vape promotion (age-restricted)
subcategories.definition: Marketing age-restricted substances in a way that targets or is accessible to minors.
subcategories.example: A vape brand sponsoring a creator whose audience is primarily under 18.

subcategories.label: Gambling promotion (unlicensed)
subcategories.definition: Promoting gambling services or platforms that lack proper licensing in the viewer's jurisdiction.
subcategories.example: A video directing viewers to an offshore sportsbook with no licensing disclosure.

subcategories.label: Prescription / pharmaceutical sales
subcategories.definition: Selling or promoting prescription medications without proper authorization or medical oversight.
subcategories.example: A video offering prescription weight-loss drugs for direct purchase without a doctor's visit.

subcategories.label: Human exploitation / trafficking
subcategories.definition: Content that facilitates, depicts, or recruits for labor or sexual trafficking of persons.
subcategories.example: A video advertising suspiciously low-cost domestic labor with indicators of forced work.

subcategories.label: Endangered wildlife / illegal animal trade
subcategories.definition: Promoting the sale, transport, or possession of protected species or products derived from them.
subcategories.example: A video showing exotic animals for sale that are listed under CITES protections.

categories.label: Misinformation & Deceptive Sync
categories.definition: Content that presents false or misleading information as fact, manipulates media to deceive, or impersonates real entities in ways that risk public harm.

subcategories.label: Health misinformation with harm potential
subcategories.definition: False medical claims that could lead viewers to delay treatment, take harmful substances, or distrust proven interventions.
subcategories.example: A video claiming a common household product cures a serious illness.

subcategories.label: Election / civic integrity misinformation
subcategories.definition: False information about voting procedures, election outcomes, or candidate eligibility that could suppress turnout or erode trust in democratic processes.
subcategories.example: A video falsely claiming a polling location has been moved on election day.

subcategories.label: Manipulated media / synthetic media undisclosed
subcategories.definition: Altered video, audio, or images presented as authentic without disclosure that they have been synthetically generated or manipulated.
subcategories.example: A deepfake video of a public figure making statements they never made, with no disclaimer.

subcategories.label: Impersonation of real people / brands
subcategories.definition: Pretending to be a real individual, company, or organization to deceive viewers or gain unwarranted trust.
subcategories.example: A channel posing as an official news network with copied branding and false reports.

subcategories.label: Conspiracy content promoting real-world harm
subcategories.definition: Conspiracy theories that have been linked to real-world violence, harassment, or dangerous behavior.
subcategories.example: A video inciting viewers to confront specific individuals accused in a debunked conspiracy.

subcategories.label: Deceptive edits / out-of-context clips
subcategories.definition: Editing footage to materially change its meaning or presenting clips without necessary context to mislead viewers.
subcategories.example: A speech clip edited to reverse the speaker's actual position on an issue.

categories.label: Spam & Manipulation
categories.definition: Content designed to artificially inflate engagement, deceive users for financial gain, or flood platforms with low-quality or repetitive material.

subcategories.label: Coordinated inauthentic behavior
subcategories.definition: Multiple accounts or channels operating in concert to amplify a message, evade bans, or manipulate trends.
subcategories.example: A network of accounts posting identical promotional videos within minutes of each other.

subcategories.label: Engagement bait / click farming
subcategories.definition: Content engineered to provoke clicks, comments, or shares through misleading thumbnails, titles, or calls to action.
subcategories.example: A video titled 'You won't believe what happened' with unrelated content and a request to 'like and share'.

subcategories.label: Scams / financial fraud
subcategories.definition: Schemes that deceive viewers into sending money, sharing financial details, or investing in fraudulent opportunities.
subcategories.example: A video promising guaranteed investment returns in exchange for an upfront payment.

subcategories.label: Repetitive / low-quality reuploads
subcategories.definition: Mass-uploading the same or near-identical content across multiple channels to farm views or evade detection.
subcategories.example: Dozens of channels uploading the same clip with slightly altered titles.

subcategories.label: Off-platform link spam to malicious destinations
subcategories.definition: Posting links that lead to phishing pages, malware downloads, or deceptive landing pages.
subcategories.example: A video description containing a shortened URL that redirects to a credential-harvesting site.

subcategories.label: Fake giveaways / lottery scams
subcategories.definition: Promising prizes, giveaways, or lottery winnings that do not exist, typically to collect personal data or money.
subcategories.example: A video claiming viewers have won a gift card and asking them to enter payment details to claim it.

categories.label: Brand Safety (GARM)
categories.definition: Content that, while not necessarily policy-violating, sits adjacent to sensitive topics and may be unsuitable for mainstream advertising placement per GARM framework guidelines.

subcategories.label: Adjacency to profanity / mature language
subcategories.definition: Content featuring frequent or severe profanity that makes it unsuitable for brand advertising.
subcategories.example: A comedy video with continuous bleeped or unbleeped strong language.

subcategories.label: Adjacency to violence (non-policy-violating)
subcategories.definition: Content referencing or depicting violence in a way that does not violate platform policy but is sensitive for advertisers.
subcategories.example: A news recap discussing a violent event with restrained but vivid descriptions.

subcategories.label: Adjacency to adult / sexual themes (non-policy-violating)
subcategories.definition: Content discussing sexual topics in a mature, non-explicit manner that may still be unsuitable for all brand contexts.
subcategories.example: A health educator discussing contraception in clinical terms.

subcategories.label: Adjacency to sensitive social/political issues
subcategories.definition: Content covering divisive social or political topics that carry reputational risk for advertisers.
subcategories.example: An opinion video on a contentious legislative debate.

subcategories.label: Adjacency to death / injury / military conflict
subcategories.definition: Content referencing death, serious injury, or armed conflict in a factual but sensitive context.
subcategories.example: A documentary clip covering a recent armed conflict with battlefield footage.

subcategories.label: Adjacency to controversial IP / hate-adjacent aesthetics
subcategories.definition: Content that borrows visual or thematic elements from controversial intellectual property or aesthetics associated with extremist movements.
subcategories.example: A music video using imagery stylistically similar to a recognized extremist symbol.

categories.label: Community Standards (Platform-Specific)
categories.definition: Content that violates house rules specific to a given platform — including quality thresholds, metadata accuracy, and community-tone expectations — but may not breach universal policy.

subcategories.label: Off-topic / wrong category
subcategories.definition: Content posted in a section or channel where it does not belong per platform guidelines.
subcategories.example: A cooking tutorial uploaded to a channel designated for educational content.

subcategories.label: Low production quality — vertical framing, unwatchable audio
subcategories.definition: Videos with technical issues — such as unwatchable audio, extreme pixelation, or unplayable encoding — that fail minimum quality thresholds.
subcategories.example: A video recorded with a broken microphone producing only static noise.

subcategories.label: Duplicate / cross-posted content
subcategories.definition: The same video uploaded multiple times to the same platform, or cross-posted without adaptation to channels that prohibit it.
subcategories.example: An identical video appearing on three channels owned by the same creator.

subcategories.label: Metadata mismatch — title/tags don't match content
subcategories.definition: Titles, descriptions, or tags that do not accurately represent the video's actual content, often used for clickbait.
subcategories.example: A video titled 'Breaking News' that is actually a product review.

subcategories.label: Community-tone violation (family-friendly surface)
subcategories.definition: Content that, while not violating universal policy, is inconsistent with a platform's stated family-friendly tone or audience expectations.
subcategories.example: A creator using innuendo and suggestive humor on a platform marketed to young audiences.
